name: RABBIT-CAGE-BACKLOG-21
title: Surface drift message should name the drifted files and what was rebuilt
description: Current message: '[🐇 rabbit 🐇] 🔄 ━━━ Surface drift detected — workspace rebuilt from sources ━━━ 🔄'. This is ambiguous — user cannot tell what drifted or what was rebuilt. Fix: the message body in rabbit-print-messages.json uses a {files} placeholder, and sync-check.py's render_surface_drift() passes the list of drifted file names (e.g. 'hooks/sync-check.py, hooks/session-init.py'). The rendered output should read e.g.: '[🐇 rabbit 🐇] 🔄 ━━━ Surface drift detected — rebuilt: hooks/sync-check.py, hooks/session-init.py ━━━ 🔄'. The drift detection already identifies which copy-file targets are out of sync (test-generated-surface.py exit code tells us drift exists; build.py knows which targets it fixed). Extract the list of rebuilt targets and surface them in the message.
